steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text claims the spoiler enjoyment gap varied in degree across stories — some stories showed a larger gap than others. The correct answer pairs two stories whose gap sizes are clearly different from each other.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge' vs. 'A Chess Problem': 'A Chess Problem' has a notably larger spoiler gap (~1.1) than 'Owl Creek Bridge' (~0.2), making the variation in effect size obvious. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The Calm' and 'Plumbing': Their gaps are ~0.7 and ~0.8 — very similar — so they do not illustrate meaningful variation in degree. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Blitzed' and 'A Chess Problem': Gaps are ~0.8 and ~1.1 — moderately close — the contrast is not nearly as clear as Choice A. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Blitzed' and 'Plumbing': Both gaps are ~0.8 — essentially identical — no variation to demonstrate. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
